_comment: Granularity (RQ1) config — three-level hierarchy for question_complexity, question_answertype, and question_linguisticvariation. Use one of the three categorization blocks at a time, or combine them. Probabilities within each categorization must sum to 1.0.

question_categorizations._comment_block: === QUESTION COMPLEXITY — choose coarse / medium / fine ===

categories.name: simple
categories.description: A question that can be answered using information from a single, localized section of the document without requiring synthesis across multiple parts.

categories.name: complex
categories.description: A question that requires synthesizing or connecting information from multiple sections of the document or making inferences beyond directly stated facts.

categories.name: expert
categories.description: A specialized user with deep understanding of technical terminology and domain concepts who expects precise, detailed information.

categories.name: novice
categories.description: A general user with limited domain knowledge who needs explanations using accessible language without specialized jargon.

categories.name: single_fact
categories.description: A question asking for one specific fact, date, name, or piece of information that appears explicitly in one location in the document.

categories.name: multi_fact_local
categories.description: A question requiring multiple facts or pieces of information that appear within the same paragraph or closely related section of the document.

categories.name: cross_section_synthesis
categories.description: A question requiring the reader to connect or synthesize information from multiple distinct sections of the document that are not immediately adjacent.

categories.name: comparative_analysis
categories.description: A question requiring comparison, contrast, or relational analysis between different entities, concepts, time periods, or perspectives discussed in the document.

categories.name: extractive_span
categories.description: A question whose answer is a single contiguous text span that can be directly copied from one location in the document without any modification.

categories.name: multi_span_aggregation
categories.description: A question requiring aggregation of multiple text spans from the same section (e.g., listing several items, combining related facts from nearby sentences).

categories.name: paraphrasing_required
categories.description: A question whose answer requires paraphrasing or restating information from the document in different words, even though the information comes from one section.

categories.name: single_hop_inference
categories.description: A question requiring one logical inference step beyond what is explicitly stated, such as identifying an implication, consequence, or reason based on stated facts.

categories.name: multi_hop_reasoning
categories.description: A question requiring multiple connected reasoning steps, such as chaining together facts from different parts of the document to reach a conclusion.

categories.name: comparative_synthesis
categories.description: A question requiring structured comparison and synthesis, such as identifying similarities/differences, trade-offs, or relationships between multiple entities or concepts across the document.

categories.name: extractive
categories.description: A question whose answer can be directly extracted as one or more contiguous text spans from the document without requiring rephrasing or synthesis.

categories.name: abstractive
categories.description: A question whose answer requires generating new text through paraphrasing, summarizing, or synthesizing information rather than extracting exact phrases from the document.

categories.name: short_span_extraction
categories.description: A question whose answer is a short phrase or single sentence that can be copied verbatim from the document.

categories.name: list_or_enumeration
categories.description: A question whose answer is a list of items, steps, or elements that requires collecting and potentially organizing information from one or more parts of the document.

categories.name: summary_or_explanation
categories.description: A question whose answer requires summarizing a concept, process, or event described in the document using condensed or rephrased language.

categories.name: synthesis_or_analysis
categories.description: A question whose answer requires synthesizing multiple pieces of information or analyzing relationships, causes, effects, or implications discussed in the document.

categories.name: exact_terminology
categories.description: A question using key terms and phrases that appear verbatim in the document, making lexical matching straightforward.

categories.name: partial_overlap
categories.description: A question where some key terms match the document while others use synonyms or related expressions, creating moderate lexical distance.

categories.name: synonym_substitution
categories.description: A question where most or all key terms are replaced with synonyms or semantically equivalent phrases not found in the document.

categories.name: conceptual_rephrase
categories.description: A question that describes the same concept or entity as the document but using entirely different vocabulary and conceptual framing.
